result:
Security audit complete. ✅

## Summary

I ran the **Harden** security audit (`ENTRY_POINT=./`, `OUTCOME_TYPE=pr`) over the full repo — shell scripts, the TypeScript deployment hook, and the 3,345-line `ralph-run.sh`.

**PR opened:** https://github.com/thabungm/testHubLaunch/pull/45 — `harden: security audit ./ (2026-06-29)`

### Fixes applied (2 files, behavior-preserving)
| Severity | CWE | Location | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| Medium | CWE-377 | `hula-verify-gather.sh`, `hula-verify-post.sh` | Predictable `/tmp/hula-verify-*-${PR_NUMBER}.*` paths in world-writable `/tmp` (symlink/clobber/info-leak) → replaced with `mktemp` (random, `0600`) |
| Low | CWE-20 | `deploymentStartupScript.ts` | Unguarded `JSON.parse` of CLI arg → wrapped in `try/catch` + validate `deploymentUrl` |

### Reviewed, no change (informational, documented in PR)
- `ralph-run.sh` `eval "$CMD"` for regression commands — operator-authored input, `eval` needed for shell operators; no safe minimal fix.
- `ralph-run.sh` remote aux-file fetch appended to agent context — prompt-injection surface, bounded to configured path.

### Clean
- **No hardcoded secrets** — all secret-shaped matches are env-var references.
- **Dependency audit N/A** — no `package.json`/lockfile (`npm audit` → ENOLOCK), so no JS dependency tree and no new critical findings introduced.

### Verification
- `bash -n` passes on all 10 shell scripts.
- TS edit reviewed (no `tsc`/strip-types on Node 20 in this env); `process.exit` is typed `never` so `context` stays definitely-assigned.

Lessons file updated throughout at `.hublaunch/lessons/harden-scan-202606290947_RALPH_LESSONS.md`.
